items.q: What is the main function of dietary fibre?
items.correct: Aiding digestion and promoting bowel health.
items.why: Students think all carbohydrates give energy. Fibre is a carb humans can't digest, so it provides NO direct energy. It bulks stools, slows glucose absorption, and feeds gut bacteria.

items.q: What is the purpose of a star profile?
items.correct: Comparing multiple sensory attributes of products visually on a radar diagram.
items.misconception: Awarding overall scores out of 5 stars.
items.why: Students confuse star profiles with rating systems. Star profiles plot attributes (sweetness, crunch, saltiness, etc.) on radial axes - the SHAPE shows the product's sensory fingerprint, not its quality rating.

items.correct: Energy used to maintain basic bodily functions at complete rest.
items.why: Students think BMR is about exercise. It's the baseline energy needed just to STAY ALIVE - breathing, heart, brain, organ function - typically 60-70% of total daily energy use even before any activity.

items.q: How many seconds for hand washing?
items.why: It's 20. “10” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
The answer examiners want is: Avoid contaminating food. “Looks neat” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What is the minimum hand-wash time in seconds?
items.why: Exact answer: 20. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
